summary: Updated the project’s dtk-template-derived tooling: tool pins, Nix derivations, build-generation support for precompiled headers and context options, docs for splits/symbols formats, and matching-status metadata affected by the toolchain update.

key_files.role: Updated tool version tags and adjusted several object Matching/NonMatching statuses.
Main dtk-template build-generator update: precompiled headers, decompctx excludes/defines, wibo platform logic, objdiff watch patterns.
Added context-generation exclude globs and macro prelude definitions.
Pinned Nix decomp-toolkit package from 1.6.2 to 1.8.3.
Updated m2c source revision/hash and adjusted the pyproject graphviz patch string.
New documentation for splits.txt sections, file attributes, and section attributes.
New documentation for symbols.txt syntax and symbol attributes.
Added editor associations for .pch and .pch++ files.

what_changed: Tool pins in configure.py moved binutils 2.42-1 to 2.42-2, compilers 20250520 to 20251118, decomp-toolkit v1.6.2 to v1.8.3, objdiff v3.0.0 to v3.6.1, and sjiswrap v1.2.1 to v1.2.2; wibo stayed at 0.7.0.
.nix/decomp-toolkit.nix was updated to decomp-toolkit 1.8.3 with a new fetchFromGitHub hash, and .nix/m2c.nix was updated to m2c commit 900b0b6a55307f6cd1270023f641c7380083d8c7 with a new hash.

tools/project.py gained a ProjectConfig.precompiled_headers field, PrecompiledHeader typing alias, get_pch_out_name(), mwcc_pch and mwcc_pch_sjis Ninja rules, .mch output generation, and pre-compile dependencies for PCH outputs.
tools/project.py now forwards ProjectConfig.context_exclude_globs and ProjectConfig.context_defines into the decompctx Ninja rule as -x and -D arguments.
tools/decompctx.py added fnmatch-based include exclusion with -x/--exclude and a generate_prelude() path for -D/--define macros at the top of generated context files.
tools/project.py broadened use_wibo() platform checks to include darwin plus aarch64/arm64, and adjusted wrapper implicit dependency handling so an existing wrapper or downloaded wibo can be tracked.
Objdiff watch patterns now include more C/C++ and header extensions, including .cc, .cxx, .c++, .hh, .hxx, .h++, .pch, and .pch++.
New docs/splits.md documents splits.txt section headers, DOL/REL address conventions, comment:0, order:, common BSS, rename:, and skip attributes.
New docs/symbols.md documents symbols.txt syntax and attributes such as type:, size:, scope:, align:, data:, hidden, force_active, noreloc, noexport, and stripped.

configure.py changed several melee/it/items object entries from Matching to NonMatching, including itheart.c, ittomato.c, itbox.c, itharisen.c, itlipstick.c, itmariocape.c, itchicorita.c, itthunder.c, and itfushigibana.c, and added itchicoritaleaf.c as NonMatching.
Several Python files received formatting-only cleanup consistent with template/style updates; tools/download_tool.py also narrowed a bare except to except ImportError.
.editorconfig now unsets indent_style for Markdown files, and VS Code settings associate .pch with C and .pch++ with C++.
smart_moves: The PCH output helper intentionally returns an absolute .mch path so Ninja can match MWCC depfile references back to the generated PCH target; the code comment explicitly calls out avoiding stale PCHs when headers change.
PCH support covers both plain MWCC and sjiswrap-wrapped builds, preserving Shift-JIS handling for projects that need it instead of treating PCH as a separate incompatible path.
Context customization was added as generated-build configuration, not ad hoc source edits: ProjectConfig.context_exclude_globs and context_defines flow into decompctx as explicit -x and -D flags.
The tool-version update was kept synchronized across configure.py and Nix package definitions, reducing the chance that local/Nix builds use different decomp-toolkit or m2c revisions.
The PR kept wibo pinned at 0.7.0 after collaborator discussion instead of upgrading it without evidence of need.
The splits and symbols documentation captures decomp-toolkit file-format details that are easy to misuse during future split/symbol work.
decomp_lessons: Toolchain/template upgrades can change progress accounting and match outcomes; rerun the decomp.dev report and update Matching/NonMatching metadata instead of assuming old status remains valid.

When adding precompiled-header support, make the generated .mch target visible to Ninja dependency resolution; otherwise depfiles may point at a filesystem path that has no corresponding rebuild rule.
Use decompctx -D to inject context-only macros and -x to skip problematic includes rather than polluting production headers for m2c/decomp context generation.

For splits.txt, use comment:0 for assembled or non-mwcc objects where generating a .comment section would break mwld, and use order: only for ambiguous link-order cases because decomp-toolkit normally resolves order automatically.

For symbols.txt, attributes such as force_active, noreloc, noexport, and stripped are matching controls, not just documentation; they affect generated objects, deadstripping, relocation interpretation, code-merging/export behavior, and common BSS handling.

Editor/tooling support should include project-specific generated/source extensions such as .pch and .pch++ so decomp work does not lose language services or rebuild watching on newer template features.
naming_conventions: Tool version fields remain named config.binutils_tag, config.compilers_tag, config.dtk_tag, config.objdiff_tag, config.sjiswrap_tag, and config.wibo_tag.
Precompiled header project configuration is named precompiled_headers, with a PrecompiledHeader alias and get_pch_out_name() helper.
PCH source extensions are .pch for C and .pch++ for C++, and generated MWCC precompiled-header outputs use .mch.
Ninja rules for PCH are named mwcc_pch and mwcc_pch_sjis, mirroring existing mwcc and mwcc_sjis naming.
Context-generation configuration is named context_exclude_globs and context_defines, mapping to decompctx -x/--exclude and -D/--define.
splits.txt uses section/file attributes such as type:, align:, vaddr:, comment:, order:, start:, end:, rename:, common, and skip.
symbols.txt uses symbol attributes such as type:, size:, scope:, align:, data:, hidden, force_active, noreloc, noexport, and stripped.
configure.py object status continues to use Object(Matching, path) and Object(NonMatching, path) entries.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use splits.txt comment:0 for assembly-only files like the documented TRK_MINNOW_DOLPHIN/ppc/Export/targsupp.s example when a generated .comment section would crash mwld.
Use splits.txt order: as a fine-tuning anchor only for ambiguous link-order resolution; the docs state decomp-toolkit normally generates link order automatically.
Use symbols.txt force_active to mark a symbol exported and add it to FORCEACTIVE in the generated ldscript.lcf when deadstripping must be prevented.
Use symbols.txt noreloc for data that looks like pointers but should not be interpreted as relocations.
Use decompctx -x include globs to leave an include visible in comments but skip importing its contents into the generated context.
Use decompctx -D definitions to create a local 'decompctx prelude' for m2c/decomp contexts.
Treat PCH inputs as build artifacts with explicit MWCC -precompile rules and include .pch/.pch++ in watch patterns so objdiff and incremental builds see them.
review_feedback: No formal review comments or reviews were present in the slice, and the PR body was null.

Human discussion focused on wibo: ribbanya asked whether to use r-burns's patched wibo; r-burns said the current approach worked and asked whether recent wibo releases were needed, noting a happy pin to 0.7.0; ribbanya replied, 'Nah, it's fine.'

The automated decomp.dev report showed linked code at 30.63% (-0.41%, -15772 bytes), matched data at 34.48% (+13.49%, +163392 bytes), linked data at 28.79% (-0.08%, -1008 bytes), 667 new matches, and 3 broken matches.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use ProjectConfig.precompiled_headers or .pch/.pch++ files.
Check whether the item TUs demoted to NonMatching in this PR were later restored to Matching.
Search for follow-up changes to wibo 0.7.0, darwin arm64 use_wibo, or r-burns's wibo patch.
Look for PRs that rely on decompctx context_exclude_globs or context_defines for m2c output.
Audit future decomp-toolkit version bumps for matching-status flips and decomp.dev report regressions.
Search for docs/splits.md and docs/symbols.md references in later split/symbol cleanup PRs.
